fbsql: PostgreSQL extension (control file + PGXS; requires plr)
postgresml: PostgreSQL extension (Rust/pgrx + bundled Python ML runtime); distributed as a ~15GB docker image with the extension preinstalled
notes: both live inside PostgreSQL; verified with 2.7.12 on the bundled PostgreSQL 15

fbsql: fbsql.fit_glm(relation SQL string; R formula; family) returning a relation
postgresml: pgml.train(project_name; task; relation_name; y_column_name; ...) returning a one-row status (project, task, algorithm, deployed)
notes: pgml snapshots the named table; every non-label column becomes a feature; automatic 25% test split (test_sampling: last) and metric computation; the best model is auto-deployed
no family/link concept; no standard errors / p-values / CIs anywhere -- metrics are ML metrics (roc_auc; f1; accuracy)

fbsql: fbsql.predict_glm(relation; model; on_new_levels) returning the scored relation
postgresml: pgml.predict(project_name; ARRAY[features]) scalar per row; pgml.predict_proba() returns a per-class probability array; ROW(...) form also accepted
notes: prediction references the PROJECT NAME whose currently deployed model is used -- an indirection through mutable state rather than a value passed by the caller

postgresml: class label (0/1) from pgml.predict; probabilities only via separate predict_proba

fbsql: single relation returned to the caller (term rows + metadata jsonb)
postgresml: serialized binary model (686 bytes for the example) in pgml.files; catalog tables pgml.projects / models / snapshots / files / deployments
notes: the model never exists as a user-visible relation

fbsql: metadata jsonb column inside the returned relation
postgresml: pgml.snapshots stores per-column type / preprocessor / statistics / category mappings in system catalog tables
notes: structured metadata exists but lives in pgml's catalogs rather than travelling with a model relation

fbsql: automatic (glm conventions: treatment contrasts; xlevels stored)
postgresml: text columns accepted; default encoder 'native' label-encodes to ordinal reals (observed: F=1.0; M=2.0; Other=3.0; __NULL__=0.0) -- imposes an artificial ordering in linear models; other encoders configurable via the preprocess argument (not verified)
notes: key statistical-semantics difference from treatment contrasts

fbsql: Complete Case Analysis at fit (n_obs/n_used/n_dropped); NULL predictors predict NULL
postgresml: fit-time imputation policy per column (default impute: error); pgml.predict raises 'ERROR: array contains NULL' on NULL features (hard error; observed)
notes: the caller must guard NULL rows by hand (CASE WHEN ... IS NOT NULL)

fbsql: yes (any SQL string defining a relation)
postgresml: partial (table/view name string; no inline subquery)

fbsql: yes (one relation from a set-returning function)
postgresml: no (train returns a status row and writes catalog state; predict is a scalar function per row)

fbsql: not applicable (relation columns keep their SQL types)
postgresml: pgml.predict feature array is a homogeneous REAL array; mixed-type rows require the ROW(...) form

fbsql: deterministic; R parity locked by pg_regress
postgresml: train/test split is deterministic (test_sampling: last) but no exposed random seed for the underlying sklearn estimators; training aborts if the test split contains a single class (observed); predictions depend on mutable deployment state
not verified (categorical predict via ROW not exercised)
